current_phase: stabilization, integration hardening, and workflow expansion
summary: BrandBuild is live on brandbuild.online with a premium dashboard, a settings control center, a compare-outputs workspace at /dashboard/reviews, an assets workspace for library browsing, version groups, and handoff packages at /dashboard/assets, and a repaired campaign-creation flow that now submits through a dedicated JSON API instead of a brittle server-action path. The auth boundary has been hardened so bad session cookies or auth callback failures redirect safely instead of triggering the global error page. Real provider smoke testing now shows that Sora can accept live jobs, Kling is blocked by account balance, Higgsfield is blocked by invalid credentials, and private uploads remain blocked on deployments that do not have SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY configured.
active_workstreams: version groups, handoff packages, and delivery workflow refinement
current_priorities: Keep brandbuild.online stable on the latest deployment.
Finish the Supabase Auth trust layer so emails and redirects are fully BrandBuild-native.
Configure SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY so private uploads work on the live deployment.
Resolve provider account readiness: fund Kling, fix Higgsfield credentials, and continue Sora validation.
Turn the new assets workspace into a true timeline sequencing and delivery surface.
blockers: Supabase Auth dashboard still needs branded email hook enablement, SMTP sender setup, and redirect cleanup.
Production does not currently have SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, so private uploads are blocked.
Kling live validation is blocked by account balance.
Higgsfield live validation is blocked by invalid credentials.
risks: The repository can fall back to demo data too aggressively when live queries partially fail, which can hide production issues.
Direct live verification for some HTTPS paths from this shell can be noisy because PowerShell/curl intermittently hit local schannel credential errors.
Multiple memory folders can drift if they are not mirrored deliberately.
Multiple local clones of the BrandBuild repo can drift if future changes are split between D:\\Playground\\brandbuild-online-repo and D:\\Projects\\brandbuild.
assumptions: BrandBuild and AI Video Studio are the same product family.
D:\Playground\brandbuild-online-repo is the canonical BrandBuild repo and deployment source.
The current production deployment should track the local clean BrandBuild workspace, not the mixed legacy root workspace.
known_issues: Branded auth email infrastructure exists in code but is not fully live until the Supabase dashboard settings are completed.
Shot uploads now tell the truth about deployment readiness, but users still cannot upload private references until SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is configured.
There is still no dedicated sequencing timeline for arranging selected outputs across a campaign.
The root package naming still says ai-video-studio while the product brand is BrandBuild.
Next.js build warns that it is inferring the workspace root because both D:\Playground and the BrandBuild repo contain lockfiles.

Added the studio system audit model and settings UI.
Recovered production by deploying the latest BrandBuild workspace to Vercel and confirming brandbuild.online points at the new deployment.
Hardened the studio persistence client so dashboard pages return null instead of throwing when server-side auth client initialization fails.

Pulled the current production environment into apps/dashboard/.env.local so local runs stop throwing NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL is not set.
Removed session-aware root-page calls so the marketing homepage is static again and the Dynamic server usage build warning no longer appears.
